mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic output expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings. A rising INDPRO trend therefore predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity prices revalue to match improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO months show negative mean forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate sustained above 9% signals severe labor market slack that compels expansionary fiscal and monetary policy, with the resulting stimulus expectations and mean-reversion in employment lifting equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of historical months when UNRATE exceeds 9%, or the mean return in those months is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and consumer prices are rising, negative real interest rates reduce the opportunity cost of holding equities and drive capital reallocation into risk assets, producing positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS generates a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the single-factor FEDFUNDS signal.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-crisis recovery, positive surprises in quarterly real GDP growth revise corporate earnings expectations upward, leading to sustained buying pressure in equities over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following positive GDPC1 growth surprises during 2009-2011 are negative on average, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over that window is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales signal robust consumer demand, which drives top-line revenue growth for consumer-facing corporations and supports positive near-term equity returns as the market prices in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean 5-day return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above the 550 thousand unit threshold mark the end of a construction slump and signal multiplier effects through durable goods and employment, generating positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 550, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional mean return is insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls expanding while the federal funds rate remains anchored near zero creates a Goldilocks environment of strong activity without imminent tightening, compressing equity risk premiums and lifting prices over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS growth and low FEDFUNDS produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform the PAYEMS-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index during the recovery raises real household disposable income and boosts near-term consumption expectations, leading to positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Months with negative CPIAUCSL change show negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean 5-day return in those months is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
